text: Iron deficiency is most common in female athletes, affecting approximately 15-35% of female athlete cohorts.
Approximately 5-11% of male athlete cohorts present with iron deficiency.
Iron is critical for oxygen transport and energy metabolism, making it especially important for endurance athletes.
Exercise-induced increases in hepcidin, the master iron regulatory hormone, contribute to altered iron metabolism in athletes.
Endurance athletes and female athletes are at highest risk for iron deficiency and should be regularly screened.
Training load, sex hormones, dietary macronutrient composition, and environmental stress such as altitude training all influence an athlete's iron status.
